creator: tijl@FreeBSD.org
text: Several ports set MASTER_SITES (e.g. to EPEL) and also want DIST_SUBDIR=centos.  The subdirectory is for all rpm targetting CentOS, not just official CentOS packages.  USE_LINUX_RPM is really tailored for linux-c?-* ports.  If you are creating such a port you can put the distfile in the centos subdirectory.  If you are creating another port which happens to be distributed as an rpm take a look at astro/google-earth.  The main difference from a regular port is that you need a do-install target.
